comparables.name: Fireflies.ai
comparables.basis: Same buyer (knowledge workers), same workflow (meeting transcription and notes), same budget (productivity tools). Fireflies has 16M+ users and a $1B valuation. Granola differentiates on no-bot approach.
comparables.oneLiner: AI meeting assistant that joins calls as a bot to transcribe, summarize, and search conversations.

comparables.name: Otter.ai
comparables.basis: Same buyer and workflow. Otter has been in market since 2016 with significant brand recognition.
comparables.oneLiner: AI meeting transcription and note-taking service that joins calls as a bot.

comparables.name: Read AI
comparables.basis: Same buyer and workflow. Read AI is also moving toward action-oriented features beyond notes.
comparables.oneLiner: AI meeting intelligence platform providing summaries, analytics, and action items.

comparables.name: Quill
comparables.oneLiner: AI meeting assistant that provides transcription, summaries, and integrations.

comparables.name: Glean
comparables.basis: Approaches the same problem from enterprise knowledge management side; potential overlap as Granola positions as a context layer.
comparables.oneLiner: Enterprise AI search and knowledge management platform.

comparables.name: Mem.ai
comparables.basis: Approaches meeting context from knowledge management angle.
comparables.oneLiner: AI-powered workspace for notes and knowledge management.

citations.title: Granola raises $125M at $1.5B valuation to turn meetings into enterprise AI context | TNW
citations.snippet: Granola, the London-based AI meeting app that records conversations without dropping a bot into the call, has raised $125 million in a Series C round led by Danny Rimer at Index Ventures, with participation from Mamoon Hamid at Kleiner Perkins. The round values the company at $1.5 billion, a sixfold increase from its $250 million valuation less than a year ago, and brings total funding to $192 million.

citations.title: Granola raises $125M, hits $1.5B valuation as it expands from meeting notetaker to enterprise AI app | TechCrunch
citations.snippet: Users might not like bots in meetings visibly taking notes, but a lot of them don't mind if an app on someone's computer is doing the transcription. That's the core reason behind Granola's popularity, which helped it secure $125 million in Series C funding led by Danny Rimer at Index Ventures, with participation from Mamoon Hamid at Kleiner Perkins. This has tipped the company's valuation to $1.5 billion, it said, up from $250 million as of the last round.

citations.title: Granola raises $125M at $1.5BN valuation - Tech.eu
citations.snippet: Granola, the AI note-taking app, has bagged a $125m funding round, elevating its valuation to $1.5bn, hitting unicorn status, it announced today.

citations.title: Granola Launches AI Workspace for Teams and Raises $43M Series B | VentureBeat
citations.snippet: The launch also coincides with the announcement that Granola has raised $43 million in Series B funding led by Nat Friedman and Daniel Gross from NFDG... Granola was founded in March 2023 by Chris Pedregal and Sam Stephenson... In May 2023 they raised a $4.25M Seed round from Lightspeed Venture Partners, betaworks and FirstMinute, and raised a further $20M Series A in October 2024, led by Spark Capital.

citations.title: AI note-taking app Granola hits $1.5B valuation, up from $250M in under a year
citations.snippet: Granola, a London-based AI note-taking app, has surged to a $1.5 billion valuation just a year after being valued at $250 million, following a fresh $125 million raise.

citations.title: After hitting a $1.5 Billion valuation, Granola's next move could be company-specific AI models
citations.snippet: The UK-based startup raised a $125 million Series C led by Index Ventures, with participation from Kleiner Perkins. Existing investors Lightspeed, Spark, and NFDG also joined. It's now raised $192 million in total.

citations.title: Granola Lands $125M to Turn Meetings Into AI Memory
citations.snippet: Granola went from a $250 million valuation to a $1.5 billion valuation in under a year. Today the AI note taking tool announced a $125 million Series C led by Danny Rimer at Index Ventures, with participation from Mamoon Hamid at Kleiner Perkins. Existing investors Lightspeed, Spark and NFDG also participated.
